name: Fegato Alla Veneziana
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Pounds young calf's liver, sliced 1/2 inch thick and soaked in milk overnight
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F.
2. In a 12 to 14-inch saute pan, heat olive oil over medium heat and add onions.
3. Slowly cook the onions until they are soft and starting to turn light golden brown, about 10 to 12 minutes. Add balsamic vinegar and continue cooking for 20 minutes until very soft and tan, caramelized.
4. Meanwhile, place pancetta rounds on a cookie sheet lined with parchment and bake 8 to 10 minutes, until crispy and brown. Remove and place on a plate with paper towels to drain.
5. Place a 12 to 14-inch saute pan over high heat. Remove liver from milk, pat dry, and dredge in seasoned flour. Pour remaining olive oil into the pan and add liver, three pieces at a time, and quickly cook until dark golden brown, about 2 to 3 minutes.
6. Turn and cook on other side for one minute. Continue cooking until all liver is done. When the last liver is out, add caramelized onions to the liver pan and splash with remaining vinegar.
7. Replace liver in the pan and cook until hot. Add parsley and put on a plate, placing pancetta over top.
Yield: 4 servings
